headlines.title: United Press Received No Message of Pope's Death
headlines.subtitle: Only Cable Companies in Service Deny Code Message Ca Be Sent-Associated Press Replies to "Beat."
headlines.fullText:
By Associated Press.
NEW YORK, Aug. 21-Wednesday afternoon Ammelation about 2:30 p. m. the United Press Association sent out a formal an- nouncement of the death of Pope Plus K. The Associated Press sent out no wach telegram at that hour, mor fo several hours thereafter.
Claim was made that the United Press had scored an Important body the statement that at 4:30. New York time (8:30 p. m. Roman time) The Dope was still alive and, that he actually Plus X, had died in Rome shortly aft- er the passing away of her brother. Shock caused by the pontiff's end was given as the cause.
It is said today that this report wan the result of a telegraphie error which patch from Rome. The pope's sister is aile although suffering from the when the pontiff was trend vesterday she was obliged to take to her bed. She Is under the care of Dr. Amicia.

headlines.title: YOU MAY SEE SPOT ON SUN: IS NOT OMEN
headlines.subtitle: Weather Man Landis Explains Big Mass of Vapor Is 40,000 Miles Across.
headlines.fullText:
TO SEE SUN SPOTS, Smoke a piece of glass and prop it at an angle parallel with the sun's angle some time in the morning or the afternoon while the sun is at an angle of less than 50 degrees. Place a piece of white paper elght inches un- der the glass and the sun cm ing through the smoked glass the paper. - United States Weather Forecaster D. 8. Landis.
A huge spot on the sun's face that has worried those who believe in omens is explained by United States Weather Forecaster Landis as one of the wan's common phenomena.
"Metallic vapors," Landis said Fri- day, "often rise from the sun as rap- idiv at 10.000 miles five seconds. They leave craters in the sun fre- quently 50,000 miles across. The lare- est sun spots on record was in 1858, when a spot 150,000 miles across was observed.
Spots on the sun have aroused the Ignorant ciamses of Europe to a tear destruction The spots have been no- tieed recently more than ever before because of Europe's war, but they can be observed on any bright day without observatory blew
The spot visible on the sun at the present time may be more than 40,000 miles broad.

headlines.title: AMERICANS WILL GET RELIEF FUND AT ONCE
headlines.fullText:
WASHINGTON, Aug. 21 .- There will be no further delay in disbursing to Americans in Europe the funda de- posited by relatives and friends with the treasury department and forward- ed aboard the cruiser Tennessee and the North Carolina. Orders have gone forward, It was announcedat The state officer and consular officer Hlebad to begin payment in accordance abroad
Explaining the delay in disbursement, It is said the bulk of the $2.500.000 ap- ra Congress we kend had arisen abroad as to the funds aboard the cruisers. It was supposed that the money was intended only for the relief of destitute Americans with- out credit or private meana. This has been cleared up and Secretary MeAdoo has arranged a credit of $500.000 upon which embassies, legations and con- sulates may draw to relieve destitute Americans
